summary.intent: Run three sequential tests (labeled 'Do 123') against a reflect/qmd knowledge base system, validating semantic query functionality on an existing wiki.
summary.outcome: Tests were blocked by qmd needing to compile llama.cpp from source on first use; a bug fix to lib/init.py was made and committed while waiting, but the qmd embed process was still running at session end.

repo: reflect init does not run an initial embed when wiki pages already exist — qmd embed must be run manually after init on pre-populated wikis
qmd spawns its own llama.cpp build per invocation if no prebuilt GPU binary is available, so concurrent qmd processes each trigger duplicate compiles
entire enable --agent claude-code installs an entire-search.md agent file during reflect init
A stop hook (stop-hook-git-check.sh) enforces committing and pushing uncommitted changes before stopping

code.finding: Fixed: reflect init was not running an initial qmd embed when the wiki already contained pages; added logic to trigger embedding on init for pre-populated wikis

workflow: Kill duplicate background processes before running a shared build step to avoid parallel compile waste
qmd update (BM25 index) is instant; qmd embed (vector embeddings) is slow on first run due to llama.cpp compilation
First-run llama.cpp compilation from source typically takes 5-10 minutes with no prebuilt binary

friction: qmd compiles llama.cpp from source on first use with no prebuilt GPU binary, blocking all query tests for an estimated 5-10 minutes
Two concurrent qmd query processes each triggered their own llama.cpp build, doubling compile work before being killed
reflect init silently skips the initial embed for existing wikis, requiring manual discovery of the gap via a runtime warning
open_items: The three tests ('Do 123') were never completed — qmd embed was still running at session end
Verify the lib/init.py fix correctly triggers qmd embed for pre-populated wikis during reflect init
Consider adding a prebuilt llama.cpp binary or caching the build artifact to avoid repeated from-source compilation
